steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The passage contrasts two findings: one study found no enduring investment increases after liberalization, while another found a significant increase in the three years following liberalization.

The key signal is this time-based contrast — 'enduring' vs. 'three years' — suggesting a temporary effect that fades over time.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice A captures this logic by stating that liberalization may provide a boost to investment that fades over time.

B misstates the studies as confirming economists’ expectations, which the text does not discuss.

C incorrectly frames three years as a delay before benefit, while D introduces an irrelevant comparison to non-liberalized countries.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
